qa_pair.question: PDB entry 4HHB is a human haemoglobin structure containing two distinct polymer entities. Identify the one corresponding to the BETA subunit, and report the UniProt accession that this entity maps to.

The oxygen-transport protein deposited as PDB entry 4HHB binds an iron-containing porphyrin cofactor that is present in four copies in the structure. Identify that cofactor's chemical component and report its InChIKey exactly as given by the Data API.
Consider the first biological assembly of PDB entry 4HHB. How many polymer chains (polymer entity instances) does that assembly contain?
PDB entry 1MBN is the classic myoglobin structure. What is the binomial (genus and species) scientific name of its source organism, as reported by the Data API?

PDB entry 1IEP is the Abl tyrosine-kinase catalytic domain in complex with the anti-cancer drug imatinib (Gleevec). Excluding any chloride ion, what is the three-letter PDB chemical component identifier of the bound inhibitor?
Compare the four PDB chemical components with identifiers HEM, ATP, NAD, and STI. Which one has the greatest molecular (formula) weight? Give its three-letter component identifier.
PDB entry 6VXX is a cryo-EM structure of the SARS-CoV-2 spike glycoprotein in its closed prefusion state, released in 2020. What is the DOI of its primary citation?
Using the Sequence Coordinates API, map the protein (polymer) entity of PDB entry 1IEP to UniProt. What single UniProt accession does it correspond to?
Using the domain/family resolver, resolve the protein module known as the "SH2 domain" to its InterPro accession (the exact-match canonical domain entry). What is the InterPro ID?
Using the disease-term resolver, resolve the genetic disease "cystic fibrosis" to its MONDO ontology identifier (the exact-match canonical term). What is the MONDO ID?
